audit_items.proof_trace: All argv[i] accesses are guarded by argc bounds checks (argc<3 in main at L300; argc<1 in run_desc at L214; argc<2 in run_call at L259; argc>=1/>=2 in run_stat at L156/L162), and the C standard guarantees argv[0..argc-1] are non-NULL strings. No heap allocations exist in the file; all objects (RpcClient, HttpAgent, stubs, controllers) are stack-allocated, so no null-return-value dereference is possible. agent.Init() return values are checked before any further use of the agent object at all three call sites.
